judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 2 is more complete: it matches Report 1 on token math and the backend-switch recommendation, but additionally flags the snapshot's staleness and the need to re-verify live limits before relying on it, which Report 1 omits entirely.

report_1.evidence: Total tokens needed: 9,360,000 tokens ... Northpeak (primary): ... Remaining: 640,000 tokens ... Shortfall: 8,720,000 tokens

report_2.evidence: Tokens required: 5,200 transcripts × 1,800 tokens/call = 9,360,000 tokens ... primary (default) | northpeak | nimbus-large | 10,000,000 | 640,000 | −8,720,000 (INSUFFICIENT)

report_1.evidence: Ridgeline (secondary): ... Remaining: 10,800,000 tokens ... Surplus: 1,440,000 tokens (115% of needed capacity) ... Critical change required: Use the secondary provider instead of the primary.

report_2.evidence: secondary | ridgeline | atlas-mini | 12,000,000 | 10,800,000 | +1,440,000 (SUFFICIENT) ... I would change the launch to use the secondary provider instead of the default

report_1.evidence: Recommended command: python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary ... This routes the work to Ridgeline (atlas-mini model) instead of Northpeak, ensuring the full dataset can be scored within the available budget.

report_2.evidence: I would change the launch to use the secondary provider instead of the default: python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary
The usage snapshot is 3+ hours old. Before launching, verify that ridgeline still has the reported 10.8M tokens available (other jobs may have consumed capacity since the 19:25 UTC snapshot).
